title: Fetching Data: Different Fetch Modes
description: Get results as objects, arrays, or custom classes.

slides.title: PDO Fetch Modes
slides.content:
# fetch() and fetchAll() Styles

PDO offers several fetch modes to control how each row is returned.

*   `PDO::FETCH_ASSOC` – associative array (column names as keys)
*   `PDO::FETCH_NUM` – indexed array
*   `PDO::FETCH_BOTH` – both (default)
*   `PDO::FETCH_OBJ` – anonymous object with properties
*   `PDO::FETCH_CLASS` – map to a specific class

You can set the default with `PDO::ATTR_DEFAULT_FETCH_MODE`.

slides.title: Fetch Modes in Action
Fetching into a Class

quizData.question: Which fetch mode returns an anonymous object?
quizData.explanation: FETCH_OBJ returns each row as an object with property names matching column names.
